title: chionanthus mala-elengi subsp. terniflorus
intro: Chionanthus mala-elengi subsp. terniflorus, also known as the Malay Mastic or False Ashoka, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Chionanthus mala-elengi subsp. terniflorus plant are used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be taken internally or applied topically to aid in recovery.
